summary:
Brief political discussion followed by Alpha Agent demo and troubleshooting. Brad fixed several bugs overnight and walked Adam through the latest features: tech stack analysis with documentation links, task board with lifecycle management (backlog → planning → queue → working → review → merged), work tree-based development, and auto-generated requirements documents stored in .alpha-agents folders per task.

Adam tested the app and encountered significant performance issues - slow loading, WebSocket reconnection problems, and a failed session creation. Brad acknowledged the WebSocket issues and noted a pending migration to Server-Side Events (SSE). Adam provided UI feedback including suggestions for tabs on long pages and a feedback mechanism for when the analyzer misses project structures. Brad mentioned the requirements generation now auto-creates documentation but the automation isn't fully connected yet.

The session was cut short due to Adam's computer performance issues (low memory). Brad committed to investigating the performance problems and the failed session UUID issue.
keyDiscussionPoints: Alpha Agent bug fixes and new features demo
Task lifecycle: backlog → planning → queue → working → review → merged
Work tree-based development workflow with PR creation
WebSocket reliability issues - migration to SSE planned
Performance problems on Adam's machine (M1, possibly low memory)
decisions: Need to migrate from WebSockets to Server-Side Events (SSE)
UI improvements deferred - focus remains on agent context accuracy
Brad will investigate performance issues and failed session creation

actionItems.action: Fix WebSocket reliability - migrate to SSE
Investigate failed session UUID creation bug
Look into performance issues with Alpha Agent app
Add user feedback mechanism for project analysis misses
Consider getting Adam a newer Mac (M4 mentioned)

followUp: Alpha Agent performance on Adam's machine
jiraNote: JIRA credentials not configured - unable to search for related tickets
